evals.name: fullstack-nextjs-hono-postgres-redis
evals.prompt: Scaffold the monorepo from this spec: skills/monorepo-scaffold/evals/files/eval1_spec.md
evals.expected_output: A working Turborepo monorepo with @taskflow/web (Next.js), @taskflow/api (Hono), @taskflow/shared packages, Podman Compose with PostgreSQL and Redis, all building and typechecking successfully
expectations: Reads and parses the spec document before scaffolding anything
Uses create-turbo CLI to initialize the Turborepo workspace with pnpm as package manager
Removes default create-turbo example packages (apps/web, apps/docs, packages/ui) and restructures to match the spec's flat packages/ layout
Creates all three packages from the spec: @taskflow/web, @taskflow/api, @taskflow/shared
Uses Next.js CLI generator (create-next-app) for the web package instead of hand-writing config
Creates a compose.yml with PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7 services with correct ports and credentials from the spec
Creates .env.example with all environment variables from the spec (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, API_PORT, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL)
Creates .env with working local dev defaults and adds .env to .gitignore
Adds infra:up, infra:down, and infra:reset scripts to root package.json using podman-compose
Wires cross-package workspace dependencies (@taskflow/api depends on @taskflow/shared, @taskflow/web depends on @taskflow/shared)
Runs turbo build and turbo typecheck as verification steps before presenting summary
Enables TypeScript strict mode in root tsconfig.json
Presents a summary listing all packages created, infrastructure services with ports, and key commands
anti_expectations: Does not add packages, frameworks, or infrastructure not mentioned in the spec
Does not hand-write package.json or tsconfig.json when a CLI generator is available for the framework
Does not skip the verification step (turbo build + turbo typecheck)
Does not leave placeholder version numbers in package.json dependencies
Does not hardcode database URLs or credentials directly in source code instead of using environment variables

evals.name: microservices-npm-bullmq-minio
evals.prompt: Set up the project from this spec: skills/monorepo-scaffold/evals/files/eval2_spec.md
evals.expected_output: A Turborepo monorepo using npm with @mediapipe/gateway (Fastify), @mediapipe/ingest, @mediapipe/transcode workers, @mediapipe/db (Drizzle), Podman Compose with PostgreSQL, Redis, and MinIO, all building successfully
expectations: Uses npm as the package manager since the spec explicitly says npm, not pnpm
Uses create-turbo CLI with --package-manager npm flag
Scaffolds all four packages from the spec: gateway, ingest, transcode, db
Uses Fastify CLI generator for the gateway package if available, otherwise creates manually with fastify dependency
Creates the ingest and transcode worker packages manually (no CLI generator) with BullMQ dependency and a minimal worker entry point
Creates the db package with drizzle-orm, drizzle-kit, and pg as dependencies
Creates compose.yml with PostgreSQL on port 5433, Redis on port 6380, and MinIO on ports 9000/9001 matching spec exactly
MinIO service in compose.yml includes the media-uploads bucket, access key, and secret key from the spec
Creates .env.example with all 7 environment variables from the spec including S3_ENDPOINT, S3_ACCESS_KEY, S3_SECRET_KEY, S3_BUCKET
All four packages have correct workspace dependency references (@mediapipe/db referenced by gateway, ingest, and transcode)
Runs install, turbo build, and turbo typecheck to verify the scaffold works
turbo.json build pipeline respects dependency graph (gateway/ingest/transcode depend on db building first)
anti_expectations: Does not default to pnpm when the spec explicitly specifies npm
Does not omit the MinIO service from compose.yml even though it's less common than PostgreSQL/Redis
Does not skip verification (turbo build + turbo typecheck must both run)
Does not create packages not listed in the spec (e.g., no shared or common package unless spec says so)

evals.name: ambiguous-spec-asks-before-guessing
evals.prompt: Bootstrap the monorepo from this spec: skills/monorepo-scaffold/evals/files/eval3_spec.md
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies that the spec is too vague and asks clarifying questions before scaffolding. It does not guess at frameworks, database choices, or package structure.
expectations: Reads the spec document and identifies it as ambiguous or incomplete
Stops and asks the user clarifying questions before creating any files or running any scaffold commands
Asks about which frameworks to use for the storefront, admin dashboard, and API (spec says 'some kind of API' with no framework)
Asks about which specific database and caching technology to use (spec says 'a database and probably caching' without specifics)
Asks about the package naming scope (spec doesn't define a workspace scope like @shopline/)
Does not default to pnpm silently — either asks or notes the default since spec doesn't specify a package manager
anti_expectations: Does not guess at architecture or make framework choices when the spec is vague
Does not run create-turbo or any scaffold commands before getting clarification from the user
Does not create a compose.yml with assumed database choices (e.g., picking PostgreSQL when spec just says 'a database')
Does not proceed with scaffolding and present results without first resolving ambiguities
